_description: Reference file: Sports data API endpoints for recruiting data. Tested and verified.

247sports._note: No API key required. Public JSON endpoints.

params.page: Page number (1-5 typical, 5 pages x 300 = 1500 recruits)

response_fields.Key: Recruitment ID (use for individual recruit lookup)
response_fields.Player.PlayerHighSchool.Name: High school name

params.recruitment_id: The Key field from recruits endpoint

cfbd._note: Requires API key. Register free at https://collegefootballdata.com/key. Use Bearer token auth.

recruiting_players.fields: id, athlete_id, recruit_type, year, ranking, name, school, committed_to, position, height, weight, stars, rating, city, state_province, country, hometown_info_latitude, hometown_info_longitude

recruiting_position_groups.fields: team, conference, position_group, avg_rating, total_rating, commits, avg_stars

transfer_portal.fields: season, first_name, last_name, position, origin, destination, transfer_date, rating, stars, eligibility

team_roster.fields: athlete_id, first_name, last_name, team, weight, height, jersey, year, position, home_city, home_state, home_country, home_latitude, home_longitude

venues.fields: id, name, capacity, grass, city, state, zip, country_code, location (lat/lng), elevation, year_constructed, dome, timezone

espn._note: No API key required. Public endpoints.

params.limit: Max results (default ~25, use 100 for full slate)

scoreboard.notes: Returns events[] with competitions, status, situation (possession/down-distance). Used for ticker, schedule, and live scores.

params.limit: Number of leaders per category (default 10, use 100 for deeper list)

stat_leaders.notes: Returns categories[] (passingYards, rushingYards, receivingYards, totalTackles, sacks, interceptions, etc.). Each category has leaders[] with athlete $ref URLs. Must follow $ref to get athlete details (name, position, team).

team_player_leaders.notes: Returns per-team stat leaders by category (passing, rushing, receiving, tackles, sacks, INTs). Leaders have athlete $ref URLs.

params.limit: Max results (use 200 to get all teams)

division_teams.notes: Returns items[] with $ref URLs containing team IDs. Useful for filtering stat leaders to FBS-only or FCS-only.

team_logo.notes: Direct image URL for team logo. Replace {espnId} with ESPN numeric team ID.

espn.scoreboard_params_note: Scoreboard supports: dates, groups (80=FBS/81=FCS), week, seasontype (2=regular/3=post), limit.
espn.recruit_fields: athlete (id, firstName, lastName, displayName, height, weight, position, hometown, highSchool), grade, status, attributes (40YD, 3CD, 20YS), schools, recruitingClass
espn.recruit_notes: List endpoint returns $ref URLs. Must follow each $ref to get full recruit data. 5,473 total recruits for 2026 class.

on3._note: No API key. __NEXT_DATA__ extraction from server-rendered pages. Rivals redirects here.
on3.pagination_info: 50 per page, 2894 total recruits for 2026 (58 pages)

ratings_array._note: Each player has ratings[] array with entries for each service
ratings_array.entry_fields: type, rating, stars, overallRank, positionRank, stateRank, fiveStarPlus

on3.industry_comparison_note: The /db/rankings/industry-comparison/ page is client-rendered (requires Puppeteer). The /rivals/rankings/industry-player/ page has the same data in __NEXT_DATA__.

rivals._note: Rivals now redirects to On3. Use On3 endpoints above.
